mechanism_narrative: A downward trend in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so a falling policy rate over the prior month predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is declining over the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of UNRATE above 8.5% do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the signal flips positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and consumer spending, which should lift near-term equity returns because earnings breadth improves across cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and inventory restocking, which supports risk appetite and cyclical earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month turns in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is negative across most restocking episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, real rates fall and liquidity conditions ease, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
regime_hint: zero lower bound or near-zero rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of inflation running materially above FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction between inflation acceleration and low policy rates is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 captures broad demand weakness and future earnings downgrades, so a negative quarterly GDP print should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth is not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX performance, or if the sign of the return relation is positive in most contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE together signal stronger labor-market breadth, which should improve earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice cyclicals quickly.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls within the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms each input series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST signals stronger housing demand and construction activity, which feeds into broader consumption and cyclical growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST surges above the stated threshold without subsequent positive 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal is consistently negative in housing expansion episodes.
